label: STG Logistics Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing
notes: Unambiguous high severity. CEO explicitly called it one of the worst freight recessions in history. Pipeline may return 'STG Logistics, Inc.' or 'STG Logistics' — use substring match.

label: Houthi fatal attack on MV True Confidence, crew killed
notes: Fatalities, direct attack on commercial vessel, 57% drop in Suez traffic. High confidence expected — unambiguous event with named vessel, crew deaths, and specific date. Pipeline might return 'Houthi rebels' or 'Houthis'; use substring match.

label: US East Coast port strike, 36 ports shut down
notes: Multi-entity article (ILA, USMX, 36 ports, Biden). entity_contains intentionally loose — 'ILA' or 'Longshoremen' are both acceptable. This is the known-ambiguous fixture; entity extraction is explicitly not reliable here and should be called out in the design note.

label: Halliburton Q4 2024 slight revenue miss ($5.61B vs $5.64B)
notes: Unambiguous low severity. $30M miss on a $5.6B quarter, stock down only 1.5%. If this returns severity >= 3, the model is miscalibrated. Cross-field check: if event_type is earnings_miss, severity must be <= 3.

label: J.B. Hunt slight revenue decline Q4 2024, management 'not satisfied'
notes: Low severity with ambiguous language. 'Not satisfied' and 'difficult environment' are emotionally charged but financially minor. Pipeline may over-index on negative sentiment rather than magnitude. Slightly lower confidence_min than 04 because the language is more hedged. Pipeline might return 'JB Hunt' — substring match on 'Hunt' is safer.

label: Houthis announce end of Red Sea shipping attacks
notes: Negation pair with 02. Same entity, opposite valence — attacks ended vs attacks with fatalities. Severity must be strictly less than 02. event_type should shift to a resolution category, not stay as geopolitical_attack.

label: Red Sea crisis general overview, modest impact so far (WEF)
notes: Hedged, expert-opinion framing. Key quote: 'harm to global economy is modest.' Confidence should be lower than 02 (specific fatality event) because this article is deliberately measured. confidence_max: 0.8 encodes that — a very high confidence score would mean the pipeline ignored the hedged language. Assert severity(07) <= severity(08).

label: Red Sea crisis full escalation, 57% drop in Suez traffic
notes: Full escalation picture: 190+ attacks, 57.5% Suez traffic drop, freight rates spiked, supply chains rerouted. Assert severity(08) >= severity(07). Same event_type family as 07 — the test is ordering, not taxonomy.

label: US East Coast port strike resolution (tentative agreement)
notes: Resolution version of 03. Same entity, strike suspended. Severity must drop strictly below 03. event_type may stay as labor_disruption (tentative resolution, not full) or shift to labor_resolution — both are acceptable per event_type_one_of.

label: Panama Canal drought restrictions, shipping backlogs
notes: Entity substitution anchor. Same event_type family as 03 (supply chain disruption) but completely different entity and cause. The test is that event_type_one_of overlaps with 03's set — severity is driven by event magnitude, not entity identity. Pipeline should NOT return ILA or port-related entities here.
